sanskrit:
जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च।
तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि॥
transliteration:
jātasya hi dhruvo mṛtyur dhruvaṃ janma mṛtasya ca |
tasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṃ śocitum arhasi ||
literal_meaning: For one who is born, death is certain; for one who is dead, birth is certain. Therefore, for what is unavoidable, you should not grieve.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s anticipatory grief by anchoring him in the impersonal law of existence: birth and death are non-negotiable events in a continuous cycle. Psychologically, this is an exposure-plus-cognitive-restructuring intervention: expose the inevitability of loss, then re-label grief as an optional, maladaptive response to a fixed datum.
psychological_state: Arjuna is frozen in anticipatory mourning; his sympathetic nervous system is on high alert, scanning for future losses rather than present realities.
trigger_scenario: A paramedic-parent hesitates to perform a life-saving intubation on a stranger’s child, fearing that the emotional cost of witnessing another child’s death will re-trigger memories of their own child’s recent passing.
anchor_text: Grieve not over the inevitable cycle of birth and death.
